source_jobs.title: Product Designer (Mid-Level)
source_jobs.description:
Hey there! Thanks for stopping by. We're Airtasker, the tech company that connects people who need to get things done with those who have the skills to do it. Our mission is to empower people to realise the full value of their skills. This doesn't just apply to those who use our product. It's also for our whole team of go-getters who bring the product to life and deliver impact to our community on a daily basis. Because we believe work (and life) is always better when you truly know and appreciate your strengths and skills.



We're on the hunt for a mid-level Product Designer who's hands-on, AI-curious, and wired for shipping. You'll join a cross-functional team to elevate the customer experience across our product, owning problem spaces from start to finish. Some days that means building the solution yourself to move fast and learn. Other days it means crafting considered designs for engineers to build when the work is more complex.



We move quickly, so this role suits someone who's resourceful inside real constraints, treats prototypes as the fastest way to learn, and has the curiosity to use AI in both the product we ship and the way they work. You'll think holistically about the product (not just the design), form views on what we're shipping and why, and bring them to the table.



You'll have a support network of product designers, product managers, your design manager and engineers to help you grow in your craft and your career, delivering work you're genuinely proud of.

Join our team and help shape the future of Airtasker!




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Own problem spaces end-to-end. Coordinate with PMs, engineers and product marketing to define the work, then carry it from research through to shipped solutions.

 - Bring a product mindset. Care about what we're shipping and why, not just how it looks, and form views on roadmap, scope, success metrics and trade-offs.

 - Build, ship, learn. Get something tangible in front of real users early, whether a rough prototype or a thin slice of the live product, and let what you learn drive the next iteration.

 - Discover creative solutions inside real constraints. Time, scope, resourcing and tech debt are part of the job, so find creative paths to deliver high-quality work at pace.

 - Look for AI opportunities in our product and your own work. Spot ways AI can help our users in their daily lives, and adopt AI in your own design process to ship faster and explore more.

 - Rapidly prototype with AI. Move from flows and wireframes through to high-fidelity designs and AI-built front-end prototypes. No coding background needed, just the curiosity to use AI to get there.

 - Develop and iterate on interfaces with the right level of craft, balancing pragmatism with quality.

 - Run user research and validate hypotheses to deeply understand problems and shape what comes next.

 - Apply and contribute to the Airtasker Design System, raising the bar with your own work and feeding improvements back where it makes sense.

 - Bring your work to the team for feedback, and use design reviews to land on the right solution and execution.




WE'D LOVE TO HEAR FROM YOU IF YOU TICK SOME OF THESE BOXES:

 - 4-5 years of experience in product design.

 - A hands-on mindset and a real desire to use AI, both in the product you ship and in how you work day-to-day.

 - Solid foundation in product design fundamentals, with a portfolio that shows growth in visual interface design.

 - Effective cross-functional collaboration with PMs, engineers, product marketing and others.

 - Comfortable shipping high-quality work inside real constraints.

 - Proficient in Figma and comfortable designing for iOS, Android and responsive web.

 - Proficient in qualitative research, from discovery and prototyping to testing and iteration.

 - Cares about outcomes, not just outputs, and forms views on the product, not just the design.

 - Familiarity with marketplace dynamics is helpful, but not required.

 - Open to gi

source_jobs.title: Associate Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
Hey there! Thanks for stopping by. We're Airtasker, the tech company that connects people who need to get things done with those who have the skills to do it. Our mission is to empower people to realise the full value of their skills. This doesn't just apply to those who use our product. It's also for our whole team of go-getters who bring the product to life and deliver impact to our community on a daily basis. Because we believe work (and life) is always better when you truly know and appreciate your strengths and skills.



We're on the hunt for an Associate Product Designer to join our Organic Acquisition team. This is one of the most exciting places to be at Airtasker right now. Organic Acquisition sits at the forefront of how we reach new Customers across AU, UK and US, and the playbook is being rewritten in real time. We're figuring out how Airtasker shows up in AI searches, building an Airtasker MCP so users can get anything done through their favourite AI, designing localised content and page experiences for each of our markets, and finding new ways to surface our community of Taskers so Customers can find exactly who they want.



This role suits someone with hustle, a real love for designing and building, and a strong visual design eye. Your PM will bring you well-defined problems to solve, and your job is to take that brief, run with it creatively, and come back quickly with directions the team can react to. You'll ideate broadly, not just polish one answer, and you'll use AI tools to build and prototype faster than you could by hand.



You'll have a support network of a design team, product managers, your design manager and engineers to help you grow in your craft and your career, delivering work you're genuinely proud of.



Join our team and help shape the future of Airtasker!




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Take a defined brief and run with it. Your PM will help define the problem to solve. Your job is to come back quickly with creative directions the team can react to, not just one polished answer.

 - Ideate fast and broadly. Generate many possible approaches using sketches, flows, wireframes, AI-built prototypes and whatever else moves the work forward fastest.

 - Bring a strong visual design eye. Apply solid visual craft and an understanding of UX patterns so what you ship looks and feels right, even on the first pass.

 - Help shape acquisition for an AI-first world. Contribute to how Airtasker appears in AI searches, the Airtasker MCP experience, and the surfaces that connect Customers with Taskers.

 - Design across three markets. Help deliver localised content and page experiences for AU, UK and US, learning the patterns that travel well across all three.

 - Use AI to build and prototype. Actively grow your fluency with AI design and prototyping tools, and use them to explore more directions than you could by hand. No coding background needed, just the curiosity to use AI to get there.

 - Ship and iterate. Where the problem calls for it, get something tangible in front of users early, whether a rough prototype or a thin slice of the live product, and use what you learn to inform the next iteration.

 - Apply the Airtasker Design System. Use it consistently, learn its patterns deeply, and flag gaps or rough edges you notice as you go.

 - Collaborate closely with your PM and engineers. Stay close to what's being built, communicate clearly, and document interactions and visual specs so what ships matches what was designed.

 - Run lightweight research. Help the team validate ideas through quick usability tests, prototype walkthroughs and on-platform observation.

 - Bring your work to the team. Present your work for feedback, ask good questions, and use design reviews to sharpen the solution and grow your craft.




WE'D LOVE TO HEAR FROM YOU IF YOU TICK SOME OF THESE BOXES:

You'll probably have 1-3 years of experience in product design, with a portfolio that shows clear visual craft and a feel for interface

source_jobs.title: Media Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Hey there! Thanks for stopping by. We’re Airtasker, the tech company that connects people who need to get things done with those who have the skills to do it.

Our mission is to empower people to realise the full value of their skills. This doesn’t just apply to those who use our product. It’s also for our whole team of go-getters who bring the product to life and deliver impact to our community on a daily basis. Because we believe work (and life) is always better when you truly know and appreciate your strengths and skills.

This is a newly created opportunity to work alongside our Global Media Strategist, supporting brand campaigns across Australia, the UK, and the US. From TV and radio to out-of-home and emerging channels, you'll help coordinate campaign delivery, reporting, and media partnerships across multiple markets.



You'll gain hands-on exposure to global ATL strategy, media mix modelling (MMM), and the execution of large-scale brand campaigns.




 


WHAT YOU'LL DO:

As Media Coordinator, you'll provide operational support to the Global Media Strategist, helping coordinate ATL media partnerships across Australia, the UK, and the US.

 - Campaign & Asset Coordination: Assist the Global Media Strategist in managing end-to-end campaign delivery. Coordinate with the in-house creative studio and external agencies to ensure media assets are delivered correctly and on time.

 - Media Partner Support: Act as an operational point of contact for media partners and vendors, helping streamline ongoing processes and scheduling meetings.

 - Reporting & Data Gathering: Own the build of ATL campaign performance reports, collating data from media partners and internal sources, constructing graphs, tables, and visualisations, and ensuring everything is accurate, sense-checked, and presentation-ready.

 - Budget & Administration: Assist with budget tracking, invoice processing, and financial reconciliation in coordination with finance and procurement teams.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work collaboratively with local leads, brand, performance, and data teams to help align media tracking with broader marketing goals.

 


WE'D LOVE TO HEAR FROM YOU IF YOU TICK SOME OF THESE BOXES:

 - 3+ years’ experience in a media agency or in-house media role, with ATL campaign experience (TV, OOH, radio or similar)

 - Strong understanding of end-to-end media workflows, including asset coordination, partner management, tracking, and reporting

 - Experience with ATL campaign reporting and data tracking, including data collation, QA, and preparation for presentation

 - Basic understanding of Media Mix Modelling (MMM) concepts

 - Strong coordination and organisational skills across multiple campaigns and markets

 - Confident communication skills with internal stakeholders and external media partners

 - Proficiency with spreadsheets and basic analytics tools

 - Ability to manage time effectively across multiple time zones

 - Exposure to AU, US, and UK media markets is a plus

 


OUR PEOPLE EXPERIENCE:

Our people team at Airtasker are dedicated to designing an industry leading people experience. This means creating an environment where you are empowered to do your best work and realise the full potential of your skills.

Thriving

 - Hybrid working - when it comes to working, there’s no “one size fits all” approach, with Airtasker you choose the environment that helps you do your best work.

 - Airtime - welcome to no Slack and meeting-free Fridays! Airtime is designed so we regularly have dedicated focus time for work and personal growth.

 - Recharge days - each quarter we have a company-wide Recharge Day, equivalent to 4 additional days of leave per year. It’s a day for everyone when the world can wait and self-care takes centre stage.

 - Wellbeing support - our Employee Assistance Program provides confidential access for our people to GPs, dieticians, counselling and mental health specialists, providing essential support wheneve

source_jobs.title: Data Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Hey there! Thanks for stopping by. We’re Airtasker, the tech company that connects people who need to get things done with those who have the skills to do it.

Our mission is to empower people to realise the full value of their skills. This doesn’t just apply to those who use our product. It’s also for our whole team of go-getters who bring the product to life and deliver impact to our community on a daily basis. Because we believe work (and life) is always better when you truly know and appreciate your strengths and skills.

We're looking for a Data Platform Engineer to help build and operate the data infrastructure powering reporting, analytics, and AI products across Airtasker.

This is a hands-on engineering role where your work has a direct line to business outcomes. You'll own meaningful pieces of the platform - clean dbt models other teams rely on, reliable Dagster pipelines that recover gracefully, and ingestion connectors that just work. You'll grow your scope across cloud infrastructure, orchestration, governance, and the AI-enabled pipelines reshaping what data teams can deliver.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Build and maintain core data pipelines: Design and operate reliable, business-ready data models and transformation pipelines using dbt, SQL, and Python to support analytics and downstream use cases.

 - Scale our data platform: Contribute to the design and operation of AWS-based data infrastructure (including Terraform), balancing scalability, reliability, and cost effectiveness.

 - Strengthen orchestration and reliability: Help build and evolve our Dagster-based orchestration platform, improving monitoring, resilience, and operational maturity.

 - Enable self-service analytics: Deliver high-quality foundational datasets and tooling that improve data accessibility and accelerate decision-making across the business.

 - Improve data quality and trust: Apply software engineering best practices (CI/CD, testing, documentation) to ensure data products are robust, auditable, and trusted.

 - Collaborate and automate: Work closely with analysts, data scientists, and engineers, and use Python to automate workflows and continuously improve the data platform, including supporting production ML use cases.




WE'D LOVE TO HEAR FROM YOU IF YOU TICK SOME OF THESE BOXES:

 - A solid understanding of the data lifecycle, from ingestion through to analysis and consumption.

 - Strong SQL skills for data modelling, transformation, and analysis.

 - Hands-on experience using dbt in a professional or personal project setting.

 - Familiarity with software engineering principles, including version control, testing, and CI/CD.

 - Experience using Python for scripting, automation, or data manipulation.

 - The ability to understand business problems and translate them into clear data models and pipelines.

 - Strong problem-solving skills and comfort working through ambiguity and evolving requirements.

 - A collaborative mindset, curiosity to learn, and a desire to deliver meaningful business outcomes through data.


BONUS POINTS

 - Experience working with a modern cloud data warehouse such as Snowflake.

 - Exposure to AWS data services such as S3, Glue, or Lambda.

 - Exposure to Data Orchestration platforms like Airflow or Dagster.

 - Familiarity with MLOps concepts or tools such as SageMaker.

 - Experience working with business stakeholders or supporting business-facing analytics and reporting.




OUR PEOPLE EXPERIENCE:

Our people team at Airtasker are dedicated to designing an industry leading people experience. This means creating an environment where you are empowered to do your best work and realise the full potential of your skills.

Thriving

 - Hybrid working - when it comes to working, there’s no “one size fits all” approach, with Airtasker you choose the environment that helps you do your best work.

 - Airtime - welcome to no Slack and meeting-free Fridays! Airtime is designed so we regularly have dedicated focus tim

source_jobs.title: People Experience Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Hey there! Thanks for stopping by. We’re Airtasker, the tech company that connects people who need to get things done with those who have the skills to do it.

Our mission is to empower people to realise the full value of their skills. This doesn’t just apply to those who use our product. It’s also for our whole team of go-getters who bring the product to life and deliver impact to our community on a daily basis. Because we believe work (and life) is always better when you truly know and appreciate your strengths and skills.

As our People Experience Specialist, you'll be at the heart of what makes Airtasker a great place to work. Sitting within our People Operations team, you'll support every stage of the employee lifecycle and help create a workplace where people genuinely thrive.

You'll partner with employees, leaders and stakeholders to deliver seamless people operations, an engaging workplace experience, and cultural moments that bring our values to life. And you'll put technology (AI included) to work, simplifying processes, lifting efficiency and making the everyday experience better for our whole team. This is a Sydney-based role reporting to our Head of People.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - People operations & employee lifecycle

 - Be the first point of contact for our people, offering timely advice and support across the whole employee lifecycle, from pre-employment through to offboarding

 - Own and continuously improve our onboarding, offboarding and reboarding experiences, keeping them engaging, compliant and true to our values

 - Coordinate lifecycle administration, including employment contracts, employment changes, probation reviews, parental leave and exits

 - Keep our employee records and HR systems accurate, with data integrity and compliance front of mind

 - Lead and support the delivery of People programs across engagement, performance, wellbeing and employee experience

 - Partner with People Business Partners and leaders to deliver People initiatives, organisational projects and lifecycle activities

 - Find smarter ways of working by reviewing our policies, processes, systems and resources, and using technology and AI to make them efficient and scalable

 - Workplace experience, culture & safety

 - Own the day-to-day running of the office, keeping it safe, welcoming, inclusive and high-performing

 - Manage workplace facilities, suppliers, kitchen operations, hospitality and office services so employees and visitors have a great experience

 - Plan and deliver company events and cultural rituals, from End of Financial Year celebrations and Values Awards to volunteering days and all-hands

 - Partner with employee-led groups like the Vibe Committee on social events, recognition programs and wellbeing initiatives

 - Keep improving the workplace experience through employee feedback, innovation and technology

 - Lead our Work Health & Safety (WHS) program, covering compliance, risk management, workplace safety and return-to-work processes

You'll be someone who genuinely enjoys making work better for others and brings 2+ years' experience in a People Operations, HR, People Experience or People & Culture role. You're organised, proactive and thrive in a fast-paced environment where no two days are the same.




WE'D LOVE TO HEAR FROM YOU IF YOU TICK SOME OF THESE BOXES:

 - A solid grounding in generalist HR across the employee lifecycle, including recruitment, onboarding and offboarding

 - Working knowledge of Australian employment legislation, the National Employment Standards (NES) and HR compliance

 - Familiarity with Work Health & Safety (WHS) obligations, including risk management, incident reporting and return-to-work

 - Experience with HR information systems (HRIS), people processes and workforce administration

 - Excellent organisational, planning and project coordination skills, and you're comfortable juggling multiple priorities

 - Strong written and verbal communication, including preparin

notes: Airtasker is a tech company with a strong product focus, but limited job postings.
